nameMeanings.name: Wiot,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Derbyshire): variant of   Went  .
Dutch: variant of   Wind  .

variants.name: Went
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Johan.
Danish and German: variant of   Wente  .
English: topographic name from Middle English went(e) ‘passage, path, way’.

nameMeanings.name: Wyatt,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

from the Middle English personal names Wiot or Wiet. These are often pet forms of Wy, from ancient Germanic Wido (see Guy , compare Wyand 1). Wy is the usual Norman French form of the name; the Parisian French form is Guy. Wiot was also used as a diminutive of William . As a surname Wyot(t) became Wyatt during the 16th century.

in Suffolk, this is sometimes a variant of Wyard, from the Middle English personal name Wiard, which is generally from Old English Wīgheard, which is composed of the elements wīg ‘battle’ + heard ‘bold, brave’. Occasionally it may be from Old French Wiard, ancient Germanic Withard (see Viard ).

variants.name: Wiatt
meanings: English (London): variant of   Wyatt  .

variants.name: Wyeth
meanings: English (Hampshire): perhaps a variant of   Wyatt  .
History: Nicholas Wyeth emigrated from Suffolk, England to Cambridge, MA, before 1645. John Wyeth (1770–1858) was born in Cambridge and became a prominent publisher and editor in Harrisburg, PA.

variants.name: Guy
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from a common Old French personal name Guy, from ancient Germanic Wido, a short form of compound names based on the element wid(u) ‘wood, forest’, Latinized as Guido .
English (of Norman origin): alternatively, an occupational name for a guide, Old French gui (a derivative of gui(d)er ‘to guide’, of ancient Germanic origin).
Altered form of French   Guay  .

Wy is the usual Norman French form of the name; the Parisian French form is Guy. Wiot was also used as a diminutive of William . As a surname Wyot(t) became Wyatt during the 16th century.

in Suffolk, this is sometimes a variant of Wyard, from the Middle English personal name Wiard, which is generally from Old English Wīgheard, which is composed of the elements wīg ‘battle’ + heard ‘bold, brave’. Occasionally it may be from Old French Wiard, ancient Germanic Withard (see Viard ).

en.lifeSummary: Achem Wiot, Wyatt, Wiatt, Wiat was born in 1193, in Mexborough, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Ralf Waiet \ Wiot \  Wyatt \ Wiatt \ Wiat and Emma Waiet. He died in 1269, in his hometown, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Achem Wiot, Wyatt, Wiatt, Wiat est né en 1193, à Mexborough, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Ralf Waiet \ Wiot \  Wyatt \ Wiatt \ Wiat et Emma Waiet. Il est décédé en 1269, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 76 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Achem Wiot, Wyatt, Wiatt, Wiat è nato il nel 1193 nel Mexborough, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Ralf Waiet \ Wiot \  Wyatt \ Wiatt \ Wiat e Emma Waiet. Morì nel 1269, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 76 anni.
